ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Ba ŋmat saa kɔɔ burgbetir na ni yoonba, ki wouŋ na nyik danu.
translated: When the elephant went to the Burgbetir stream and drank water, it also drank mud.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: N baar ma n tan tukin bonjabike nan u baa, bonpobik nan u naa, poo nan u siponaa.
translated: When I go to the farm, I see the bonjabike tree, the bonpobik tree, and the siponaa tree.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Ŋaan digbangbeŋŋa amii digbanbis nbae ni ki i sa kɔɔ, yin boi ki la popeendaanɔ ŋaaka, ki kɔɔ kii be leŋ nan i fiiru daar.
translated: When the sun rises, the children go to school, and when they come back, they help their mothers with household chores.

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: ‘Fin Betlehem nba be Juda tingbouŋ ni na, a ki tee tinbik kaa Juda yiar ni, kimaan batsɔɔ tan sa nyi a nie, ki ŋɔɔe tan sii yen n niib Israel teeb.’ ”
translated: Now Bethlehem, in the district of Judah, was not far from Jerusalem, as the prophet had said, where it is written by the prophet, 'And you, O Bethlehem, who are in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah;'

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Yisa nba din la niburchiauŋ maŋ na, ki u doo kunkonn paak ki saa kar; ki u poorpoweiteeb lakin u boor ni.
translated: When the moon is full, we gather and go to the farm to harvest; we go with cutlasses and baskets to carry the harvest.
